# protonpass_item_login (Resource)

Manages a Proton Pass login item. Requires Terraform >= 1.11 for write-only attribute support.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "protonpass_item_login" "example" {
  share_id            = protonpass_vault.example.share_id
  title               = "My Login"
  username            = "user"
  password_wo         = "secret"
  password_wo_version = 1
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `share_id` (String) Share ID of the vault where this item is stored.
- `title` (String) Title of the login item.

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `email` (String) Email associated with the login.
- `password_wo` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Password for the login (write-only, never stored in state).
- `password_wo_version` (Number) Increment this value to trigger a password rotation. The password is re-applied only when this version changes.
- `urls` (List of String) URLs associated with the login item.
- `username` (String) Username for the login.

### Read-Only

- `create_time` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `item_id` (String) Unique identifier of the item (generated by Proton Pass).
- `modify_time` (String) Last modification timestamp.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Login items can be imported by specifying the share ID and item ID separated by a colon
terraform import protonpass_item_login.example "share_id:item_id"
```
